danger_signals.visual_form: Anthropomorphic food creatures (Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, Mr. Egg) moving through maze
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; contact causes loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy food item positioned on player's current ingredient or level
danger_signals.meaning: Collision imminent; player must flee or use pepper defense

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited pepper shots available (starts with five)
danger_signals.meaning: Defensive resource scarcity; increased vulnerability to enemy contact

danger_signals.signal: Falling ingredient with enemy on it
danger_signals.visual_form: Ingredient dropping while enemy stands upon it
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk scenario; crushing enemy awards points but ingredient falls further

safety_signals.visual_form: Vertical ladder paths connecting platform levels
safety_signals.meaning: Escape route; allows Peter to move vertically away from threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy in pepper cloud animation, briefly immobilized
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage guaranteed; Peter can move through without collision

safety_signals.visual_form: Food items (ice cream, coffee, fries) appearing in maze after ingredient drops
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to collect; awards points and restores pepper ammunition

safety_signals.visual_form: Fully stacked burger ingredients resting on waiting plate below maze
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward level completion; one objective secured

safety_signals.visual_form: Spatial separation between Peter and all three enemy types
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window; opportunity to advance objectives

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chef character sprite; controlled by player input
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Position on platforms and ladders; pepper cloud when weapon deployed

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Three distinct enemy types: Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, Mr. Egg
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each enemy has unique visual form reflecting food type; all pursue player through maze
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wander maze paths; respawn at edges when crushed or at drop points when falling; climb back up if necessary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer burgers/ingredients displayed; simpler platform maze layout; three enemies present but sparse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move at predictable pace; Peter has more escape routes; pepper shots sufficient for early threat management

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased burger count; more complex labyrinthine platform arrangement; three enemies still pursuing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies more coordinated; Peter increasingly cornered; tactical pepper use becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maximum burger ingredients; maze designed to trap Peter; all three enemies actively hunting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies coordinate paths to block escape; Peter must use complex dropping strategies and enemy-crushing mechanics; significantly higher difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Burger component drops visually through levels; collision with enemies or other ingredients on impact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies crushed under falling ingredient respawn at edges; if dropped with enemies on them, fall additional two levels per enemy, awarding bonus points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All burger ingredients stacked on waiting plate; maze clears or transitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Board advances; cycle repeats with increased difficulty; six boards form one complete cycle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cloud effect emerges from Peter in facing direction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Any enemy in cloud animation is stunned and immobilized; Peter gains brief safe passage window; pepper count decreases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All lives exhausted after repeated enemy contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game ends; restart required; one life lost per undefended enemy collision

environmental_storytelling.example: Oversized burger components (bun, meat patty, tomato, lettuce, cheese) arranged vertically communicate food construction theme; player physically assembles burgers through gravity and platforming
Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, Mr. Egg as sentient food antagonists establish whimsical food-vs-chef conflict; visual distinctness aids player threat recognition
Platform layout becomes increasingly complex across six boards; visual density increases, trap opportunities for Peter multiply; environmental design communicates escalating challenge
Ice cream, coffee, fries appear after ingredient drop milestones; visual pop-in signals reward opportunity and resource restoration (pepper shots)
Pepper count displayed as limited ammunition; visual feedback on each shot use; forces player to manage defensive resource strategically

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Wanders maze autonomously; pursues Peter when proximity allows; collision causes loss of life if Peter undefended
Wanders maze autonomously; pursues Peter when proximity allows; collision causes loss of life if Peter undefended
Wanders maze autonomously; pursues Peter when proximity allows; collision causes loss of life if Peter undefended
Returns to maze after short delay; respawns at maze edges; does not climb immediately
Falls additional two levels per enemy on ingredient; respawns at landing point; climbs back up into maze if necessary; awards bonus points
Briefly immobilized; cannot collide with Peter; effect is temporary; resumes pursuit after stun animation ends
Loses one life; respawns at starting position; game ends when all lives exhausted
Remains on ingredient as it drops; falls with component to level below
Appear in maze; grant points and extra pepper shot when collected; award bonus points to incentivize pickup

notes: BurgerTime employs simple but effective visual language for a 1987 NES port. The game communicates danger primarily through enemy proximity and scarcity of defensive resources (pepper). Safety signals are spatial (ladder access, distance from enemies) and temporary (stunned enemy windows). The world-state is conveyed through maze complexity increase and ingredient density rather than environmental narrative. Faction dynamics are minimal—a simple chef vs. sentient food opposition with no political dimension. Visual manifestation of game state relies heavily on sprite positioning, ingredient arrangement, and enemy behavior patterns. The paradigm is purely mechanical/ludic rather than narrative or political.
sources_used: tcrf - BurgerTime NES/Famicom Disk System page
